act_title: EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT 1963
content:
4.  Notwithstanding anything contained in any law for the time being
in force, no competent authority shall—
(a)  register any company, firm or other body or persons
under any name;
(b)  register a trade mark or design which bears any emblem,
name,
photograph,
drawing
or
other
pictorial
representation; or
(c)  grant a patent in respect of an invention which bears a
title containing any emblem or name,
if the use of such name, emblem, photograph, drawing or pictorial
representation would be in contravention of section 3.
Penalty

act_title: EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT 1963
content:
1.  The standards, coats-of-arms and official seals of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong
and the Raja Permaisuri Agong.

act_title: EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT 1963
content:
2.  In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—
“competent authority” means any authority competent under any
law for the time being in force to register any company, firm or other
body of persons, or any trade mark or design, or to grant a patent;
“specified emblem” means any emblem, seal, flag, pennant,
insignia, formation sign, ensign or coat-of-arms specified in Part I of
the Schedule;
6                                   Laws of Malaysia                              ACT 414
“specified name” means any name specified in Part II of the
Schedule and includes any abbreviation of any such name.
Prohibition of improper use of emblems, names and pictorial
representations

act_title: EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT 1963
content:
3.  The name of the International Criminal Police Organization which is also
known as “Interpol” or “ICPO-Interpol”.

act_title: EMBLEMS AND NAMES (PREVENTION OF IMPROPER USE) ACT 1963
content:
5. The name of the Malaysian Flag – Jalur Gemilang.

content:

known as “Interpol” or “ICPO-Interpol”.

content:

add to or alter the Schedule.

content:
